opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My whole mouth is covered in ulcers and it burns so much I can't even swallow a sip of water. I feel incredibly weak.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been getting worse over the last couple of days and I haven't been able to drink anything since yesterday evening because it hurts too much to swallow — my spouse is really worried about me.

symptom_narrative.onset: felt generally run-down with a mild fever for about 3 days, then 2 days ago got an intense tingling and burning on my lips, and it quickly turned into blisters and then raw sores all over my lips and inside my mouth
symptom_narrative.duration: about 2 days of the sores, on top of 3 days of feeling unwell beforehand
symptom_narrative.character: the lips came up in little blisters that broke and left raw, weepy sores with crusts that bleed a bit, and the inside of my mouth and gums are red, swollen and covered in painful ulcers
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is unbearable, especially trying to swallow — it's the worst pain I've ever had in my mouth
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my lips, my gums, the inside of my cheeks and my tongue — pretty much my whole mouth
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and steadily getting worse, no relief
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; cold things numb it for a second but swallowing still hurts too much
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: swallowing, eating, anything warm, spicy or acidic
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel weak, feverish and wiped out; I keep having to spit into a tissue because it hurts too much to swallow my own saliva; I've barely passed any urine
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never had anything like this before — not once
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been stuck in bed, I can't eat or drink, and I feel too weak to do anything

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications, and I haven't started any new tablets, creams or medicines recently

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: a young adult who normally looks after myself independently; my spouse is here with me and made this call because I've gone downhill and can't drink.

objective_data_offered.value: my lips are covered in broken blisters and raw sores with crusts that bleed a little, and when I open my mouth on camera you can see my gums are bright red and swollen and there are lots of little ulcers on my cheeks and tongue
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described and shown by the patient over video; not a clinician examination of the mouth or throat

objective_data_offered.value: I haven't managed to drink anything at all since about yesterday evening — roughly 18 hours — because swallowing hurts too much, and I keep spitting saliva into a tissue rather than swallow it
I've barely been to the toilet — hardly any urine — my mouth feels bone dry and I feel weak and a bit lightheaded when I sit up
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own impression of urine output and dryness; no measurement

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she looks pale, exhausted and hot, is staying in bed, and is drooling into a tissue because it hurts to swallow
I feel hot and feverish and have done for a few days, but I haven't taken my temperature with a thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken
